channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ¿Qué pasa en una crisis de petróleo? Asia tiene la respuesta
item.description: Como China y otros grandes proveedores de petróleo refinado de la región restringen las exportaciones, los países dependientes de las importaciones están desesperados por asegurarse el suministro de combustible.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson, Aaron Krolik, Tung Ngo and Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:description: Gente esperando un nuevo suministro de tanques de gas licuado de petróleo en Lucknow, India, el jueves.

item.title: Un amigo de Trump pidió al ICE que detuviera a la madre de su hijo
item.description: Paolo Zampolli, exagente de modelos y viejo aliado de Trump, estaba inmerso en una batalla por la custodia de su hijo. Un funcionario del ICE accedió a ayudarlo.
item.dc:creator: Megan Twohey, Shawn McCreesh and Hamed Aleaziz

item.title: Sanders Will Rally for N.Y. Tax Increase. Mamdani Does Not Plan to Join.
item.description: Senator Bernie Sanders of Vermont will headline a rally in the Bronx next weekend meant to increase pressure on Gov. Kathy Hochul to raise taxes on the rich.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos
item.category: Our Time for an Affordable NYC (Nonprofit)

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Bernie Sanders, left, and Mayor Zohran Mamdani are ideological allies who have made several appearances together, including last year at a “Fighting Oligarchy” event in Brooklyn.

item.title: Lo siento, Mamá. Estás chateando con un agente de IA, no con tu hijo
item.description: Los jóvenes programadores de Silicon Valley se están volviendo creativos con los asistentes virtuales. Los utilizan hasta para responder sus chats familiares.
item.dc:creator: Natallie Rocha and Arsenii Vaselenko

item.title: The ‘Hunger Games,’ Hamptons-Style: Hiring a Private Chef for Summer
item.description: On New York’s eastern shore, the wealthy are already competing to secure a chef for the season.
item.dc:creator: Dionne Searcey

media:content.media:description: Elena Apostolides, center in blue, hosts gatherings for private chefs at her home in East Hampton.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 21, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Fearing Chaos of Climate Change, Some Seek Answers in Virtual Classroom
item.description: A monthlong seminar offers lessons in how to anticipate and prepare for the mounting disruptions of global warming
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard

media:content.media:credit: Mark Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Alex Steffen, a futurist and former journalist, knew people were hungry for advice on navigating climate change. So he started a class.

item.title: One Year After Trump’s $400 Million Ultimatum, a Different Columbia
item.description: As they sought to respond to charges of antisemitism and harassment on campus, administrators and trustees made promises. Many have been kept, but not all.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Otterman

media:content.media:credit: Marco Postigo Storel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Trump administration froze $400 million in federal research funding for Columbia University last March.

item.title: Ahora cualquiera puede programar. Solo necesitas de la IA
item.description: En la era de los agentes de IA, muchos desarrolladores de Silicon Valley apenas programan. En su lugar, están haciendo algo muy, muy extraño.
item.dc:creator: Clive Thompson

media:content.media:description: Grace Hopper, pionera de la informática y partidaria de crear lenguajes informáticos más fáciles de usar, programaba problemas en cinta perforada.

item.title: Chelsea Chief Under Abramovich Could Face Scrutiny Over Soccer Cheating
item.description: A former key aide to the Russian oligarch would face an investigation into her time overseeing the Premier League club should she return to soccer.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja

media:content.media:description: Marina Granovskaia before a match at Wembley Stadium in London in 2022, when she was a Chelsea board member.

item.title: A Public Holiday Every Week? Sri Lankans Bemoan the Enforced Day Off.
item.description: The island’s government, rationing fuel amid shortages from the Iran war, surprised residents by imposing an unusual four-day week.
item.dc:creator: Pamodi Waravita and Anupreeta Das
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A vendor waiting for customers at a railway station in Colombo, Sri Lanka, on Wednesday, after the government announced a weekly holiday on Wednesdays to save fuel.

item.title: Corrections: March 21, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, March 21, 2026.

item.title: Copenhagen Grapples With the Abuse Allegations Against Noma’s Chef
item.description: René Redzepi faced swift fallout in the United States following reports of violence. But in Denmark, where he is a major cultural figure, the reaction has been more muted.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg

media:content.media:credit: Soeren Bidstrup/Ritzau Scanpix, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Through his work at Noma in Copenhagen, the chef René Redzepi, right, helped elevate Denmark’s cultural reputation on the global stage.

item.title: Laid Off in Midlife, China’s Reform Generation Braces for Downward Mobility
item.description: The future once seemed boundless for those who grew up during China’s reform era. Now in middle age, they are pinned between economic stagnation and institutional age discrimination.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan

item.title: Quote of the Day: Nonhumans Are Pitching Supplements
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, March 21, 2026.

item.title: ​At Least 10 Killed in South Korean Factory Fire
item.description: Survivors jumped from windows to escape the blaze, the deadliest at a factory in South Korea in two years. Four people are still missing.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

media:content.media:description: Firefighters spraying water to extinguish a fire at a factory in Daejeon, South Korea, on Friday.

item.title: San Francisco Driver Gets Probation After Killing a Family of 4
item.description: Mary Fong Lau, 80, will perform 200 hours of community service as part of her sentence. Her speeding vehicle crashed into a family as they waited for a bus ride to the zoo in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight

media:content.media:credit: Jessica Christian/San Francisco Chronicle, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Mary Fong Lau at the Hall of Justice in San Francisco in February.

item.title: ‘Gadzooks!’
item.description: Boaz Moser presents a lofty, themeless challenge.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Timothy A. Clary/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Poppin’ fresh, just like today’s puzzle, and stuffed with 12,000 cubic feet of expensive helium.

item.title: Nicholas Brendon, Beloved Sidekick on ‘Buffy the Vampire Slayer,’ Dies at 54
item.description: He played the part of Xander Harris, one of Buffy’s closest friends, on the hit television show about a teenage girl who protects the world from monsters.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler and Francesca Regalado

media:content.media:description: Nicholas Brendon, who rose to fame as the lovable sidekick on the hit show “Buffy the Vampire Slayer,” has died.

item.title: Striking Down Pentagon Press Limits, Judge Vindicates Independent Journalism
item.description: The ruling cut deeper than left-versus-right politics, declaring that the policy imposed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Reporters carried their belongings as they left the Pentagon after turning in their press passes last year.

item.title: Trump Signals Retreat From Goal of Regime Change in Iran
item.description: In the first days of the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran, Mr. Trump pressed for a popular uprising against Tehran’s government.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People at an apartment building that was hit by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran last week.

item.title: The Road to the Battle of Hastings in 1066? Maybe Not a Road After All.
item.description: New research suggests that a legendary 200-mile march by Harold Godwinson, the last Anglo-Saxon king of England, may have actually involved some ships.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: Harold II, King of England (Harold Godwinson)

media:content.media:description: Re-enactors setting out from Clifford’s Tower in York, England, to follow the supposed journey of King Harold to the site of the Battle of Hastings, for the 950th anniversary of the battle in 2016.

item.title: U.S. Pauses Sanctions on Some Iranian Oil to Get More to Market
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent estimated that the move would add about 140 million barrels of crude to the oil market.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a post on X that Iran would see little economic benefit from the temporary removal of the sanctions.

item.title: 700 Dogs and Cats Are Rescued in Animal Cruelty Raid in Los Angeles County
item.description: Animal control officers responded to a pet-adoption facility in Lake Hughes, Calif., after receiving complaints about neglect and overcrowding. No charges have been filed.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

item.title: A Look at the Escalating Battle for the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: There are several ways the United States could attempt to reopen the waterway, all of which carry substantial risks.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: The village of Kumzar, along the Strait of Hormuz in Oman.

item.title: ‘The Bachelorette’ Took a Risk on Taylor Frankie Paul. It Backfired.
item.description: Ms. Paul’s built-in fan base and viral interpersonal drama presented an opportunity for the long-running reality TV show, until video of a past assault derailed the season.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs and John Koblin
item.category: Mormons (Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints)
The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives (TV Program)

item.title: What Does the California Heat Mean for Fire Season?
item.description: The state is warming up and drying out earlier than usual. Here’s what that means for fire season.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

item.title: Fresno Took 30 Years to Name Cesar Chavez Blvd. Undoing It Took a Day.
item.description: The reckoning for the labor leader’s sex abuse was swift in Fresno, Calif., the heart of the Central Valley region where the farmworker movement was born. Towns across the nation face similar debates.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

item.title: ‘Walker Texas Ranger’ Star Chuck Norris Dies at 86
item.description: The family of Chuck Norris announced that the action movie star died on Thursday after he had been hospitalized for a medical emergency earlier that day. Norris was known for his martial arts skills and his role in the CBS television series, “Walker, Texas Ranger.” He was 86.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:description: Chuck Norris in 1995 as Cordell Walker on “Walker, Texas Ranger.” The series aired on CBS from 1993 to 2001.

item.title: Concierto de regreso de BTS: te decimos cómo y cuándo verlo
item.description: El primer concierto del supergrupo de k-pop en más de tres años se retransmitirá en directo por Netflix el sábado a las 7 a. m., hora del este de Estados Unidos.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang

media:content.media:description: Una pantalla cerca de la plaza Gwanghwamun de Seúl promocionando el jueves el concierto de regreso de BTS.

item.title: The Best Women’s Loafers, at a Range of Prices
item.description: Stylish and practical, these are shoes for all seasons — and all outfits.
item.dc:creator: Angela Koh

item.title: ‘We’ve Been Waiting for Years’: BTS Fans Celebrate Before Comeback Concert
item.description: Fans of BTS, the K-pop supergroup, gathered in Seoul for the boy band’s highly-anticipated comeback concert. The group released their 10th studio album on Friday after taking a three-year hiatus.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Drones form the image of Jin, a BTS member, in a light show over Seoul on Friday.

item.title: Inside the Arrest That Led to Banksy’s Possible Unmasking Decades Later
item.description: In 2000, Banksy was a largely unknown street artist hanging around downtown Manhattan when the police nabbed him for trying to deface a billboard.
item.dc:creator: Corey Kilgannon

media:content.media:description: Banksy’s “Flower Thrower” as shown at the Banksy Museum in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in Iran in Its Third Week
item.description: The Persian new year and the end of the holy month of Ramadan were marked by continuing strikes in the region as the war reached the end of its third week.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Muslims celebrate Eid al-Fitr at the Mohammad Al-Amin Mosque in Beirut, Lebanon, on Friday.

item.title: Elon Musk Is Responsible for Some Twitter Investor Losses, Jury Finds
item.description: The verdict was a rare loss for Mr. Musk, who investors claimed had tried to drive down the share price of the social media company, now called X, to renegotiate his $44 billion offer.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger and Natallie Rocha

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Elon Musk arriving at the Phillip Burton Federal Building and U.S. Courthouse in San Francisco in March.

item.title: U.S. Steps Up Attacks to Clear the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Also, Chuck Norris dies at 86. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: Ships sailing toward the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of the United Arab Emirates yesterday.

item.title: Read the Ruling in The Times’s Lawsuit Against the Pentagon
item.description: A federal judge on Friday threw out parts of the Pentagon’s restrictions on news outlets, ruling that they violated the First Amendment. The decision handed a victory to The New York Times, which had sued over the restrictions.
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Judge Rules Pentagon Restrictions on Press Are Unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge tossed parts of the Pentagon’s restrictions on news outlets, saying they violated the First Amendment, in a lawsuit brought by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A federal judge ordered the press passes of seven journalists for The New York Times to be restored.

item.title: Lo que debes saber sobre viajar a Cuba en estos momentos
item.description: El país caribeño depende del turismo, pero la falta de combustible y los grandes cortes de electricidad están obligando a muchos viajeros a cancelar sus visitas. Esto es a lo que podrían enfrentarse.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Sims

media:content.media:description: Algunos de los coches clásicos por los que se conoce a Cuba ya no dan paseos a los turistas porque no hay suficiente gasolina.

item.title: David Botstein, Gene-Mapping Pioneer, Dies at 83
item.description: His method of locating genes in human DNA allowed researchers to find disease-causing genes, and later to map the entire, sprawling human genome.
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata

item.title: California Could Elect a Republican Governor Thanks to the ‘Jungle Primary’
item.description: The state’s wide-open “jungle primary” has Democrats on edge.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:description: From left, Xavier Becerra, a Democrat, Steve Hilton, a Republican, and Matt Mahan, a Democrat, are part of a large field in the California governor’s race.

item.title: Naomi Klein on the Fascism of Elite Backlash
item.description: On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the writer Naomi Klein and the Times columnist Ezra Klein discuss how they are thinking about fascism in this political moment — from the elite backlash against movements like #MeToo to the aesthetics and impulsivity of Trumpism.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: ‘The Doppelganger Is at the Wheel’
item.description: Naomi Klein’s book “Doppelganger” is a journey into the world of MAGA and right-wing conspiracies. But it’s also a look at how the left’s political culture sits in relation to this mirror world. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the writer talks to the Times Opinion columnist Ezra Klein about how the use of the “mute button” on social media has spilled offline as well, creating political communities that are isolated from one another.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Influencers Caught in ‘Bachelorette’ Fallout After ABC Pulls New Season
item.description: When ABC abruptly pulled a planned season of “The Bachelorette,” some whose livelihoods are wrapped up in the series were thrown for a loop.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: Don DeLillo’s Hockey Novel, ‘Amazons,’ Will Return to Stores
item.description: You’re welcome.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Alter

media:content.media:description: Don DeLillo in 1997.

item.title: Chuck Norris: A Life in Pictures
item.description: The actor, known for his skills as a martial artist, often played the warrior onscreen.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Attacks Iranian Targets to Try to Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Israeli military said it had killed the spokesman for Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, and Iran warned that it could target American and Israeli military personnel.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Eric Schmitt, Francesca Regalado and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz to most Western ships, helping to drive up oil prices and rocking the global economy.

item.title: Chuck Norris Punched This Article Into the Sun: How an Action Hero Became a Meme
item.description: Norris, best known as the butt-kicking star of action films, became an unwitting if good-natured pioneer of the internet meme.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb

media:content.media:description: Chuck Norris in “Missing in Action” (1984). He once beat the sun in a staring contest, according to an internet meme from the mid-2000s.

item.title: Un barco con ayuda humanitaria se dirige a Cuba
item.description: La flotilla ha movilizado a cientos de voluntarios de más de 30 países. Algunos exiliados cubanos lo ven con recelo.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Paul Antoine Matos

media:content.media:description: Un grupo de personas observa la puesta de sol en La Habana el lunes durante un apagón.

item.title: Switzerland Blocks Arms Sales to U.S. Over Iran War
item.description: Swiss law prohibits selling war materiel to countries involved in armed conflict, a policy the country has already cited to block sales to Israel and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Nick Cumming-Bruce
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The United States is one of the biggest markets for Swiss weapons exports, including small arms.
